summary: Unit tests (tests/bump.bats) are well-structured with complete inline fixtures, covering main requirements (read, bump, write, changelog). Workflow tests (tests/workflow.bats) have critical flaw: reference external fixture files (fixtures/commits-{feat,fix,breaking}.txt) that are never created or provided, making those tests non-runnable. Unit tests also lack edge cases: no tests for empty commits files, malformed input, version edge cases (0.0.0), large numbers, scope parsing, or multiple same-type commits. Workflow tests lack the actual workflow YAML file definition. Overall: solid structure, incomplete fixtures, gaps in edge case coverage, would fail if executed without fixture files.
